## Objective: Land 3 retainer clients (anchor: Demand Generation)
- KR: qualified conversations 2/mo → 6/mo (current: 3)
- System: Monday 9-11am outreach block (trigger: time — recurring calendar block)
- Contract: countermetric — delivery quality (client NPS stays ≥ 8); measurement delay ~3 weeks
setup.journal_md:
# Journal

- **[2026-07-13] weekly**
  - Land 3 retainer clients: executed: yes; progressing: yes
  - mitigations checked: none fired
  - change: none
- **[2026-07-12] daily** — location: kitchen. Wrote: retainer objective.

user_messages.content: Time for my Monday pulse.
Honestly it's been a rough stretch. Where do we even pick up?

must_include: recognizes the ~6-week gap from the file dates
offers the Restart Protocol rather than running a routine pulse as if nothing happened
no guilt — falling off framed as data
must_not_include: a routine weekly pulse executed with no acknowledgment of the gap
a lecture about consistency
narration of state files or internal routing
must_not_do: trust chat memory over the file dates
shame the user for the lapse

tone_notes: Same advisor walking back in — names the gap plainly from the record, offers the restart as the smart next move, zero guilt.

setup.active_md:
# Active Goals

## Objective: Land 3 retainer clients (anchor: Demand Generation)
- KRs: qualified conversations 6/mo; 3 signed retainers by Sep 30

## Objective: Cut delivery rework to <10% (anchor: Delivery)
- KRs: rework rate 25% → <10%

## Objective: 20% net margin (anchor: Profit)
- KRs: margin 12% → 20%
setup.scorecard_md:
# Anchor Areas Scorecard

## Active Anchor Areas
- Demand Generation (4), Delivery (5), Profit (4)

user_messages.content: I want to add a fourth objective: launch a podcast to build my personal brand. It's important.
Come on, it's small. Just add it alongside the other three.
Fine — if I can only have three, I'd trade out the margin goal for the podcast.

must_include: refuses the fourth objective (not a warning)
records the podcast as a backlog candidate
shows the conflict — a fourth means one of the three goes
facilitates the swap as an explicit decision requiring formal closure of the margin objective (disposition recorded)
must_not_include: four active objectives in any state file
the podcast candidate silently dropped with no record
must_not_do: weaken the refusal into a warning
perform the swap without closing the displaced objective

tone_notes: Firm on the cap, generous with the mechanism — the user hears what the swap costs and decides; everything leaves a trace.

setup.journal_md:
# Journal

- **[2026-07-28] weekly**
  - Land 3 retainer clients: executed: yes; progressing: no — flagged for monthly
  - mitigations checked: none fired
  - change: none

user_messages.content: Monthly review. The retainer goal is behind — let's just make it 1 signed retainer by Sep 30 instead of 3. Update the goal.
Reason? It was too ambitious. Just change the number.

must_include: a revision record preserving the original target (3), the actual at change (0), the date, and a reason
reference to the contract's legitimate-revision conditions before capturing
must_not_include: the KR silently rewritten with the original gone
the revision refused outright — the target is the user's call
must_not_do: block the revision after the push

tone_notes: Pushes once with the record — executed-and-flat points at goal fit, and 'too ambitious' gets examined against the revision conditions — then records the user's call in full.

setup.active_md:
# Active Goals

## Objective: Land 3 retainer clients (anchor: Demand Generation)
- System: Monday outreach block

## Objective: Cut delivery rework to <10% (anchor: Delivery)
- System: Friday QA review hour

## Objective: 20% net margin (anchor: Profit)
- System: monthly pricing audit, first Tuesday

user_messages.content: Pulse: outreach ran and I booked two conversations. The QA hour didn't happen, client fire on Friday.
The margin one... I honestly don't know, I haven't looked at the numbers.

must_include: three separate per-goal records: outreach yes/yes, QA no, margin unknown
the unknown recorded as unknown and named plainly ('no read this week')
must_not_include: one aggregate yes/no for the week
the margin answer inferred to yes or no from tone
must_not_do: guilt the user over the unknown
expand the pulse into a coaching session

tone_notes: Five minutes, three clean records — the mixed week is representable, the unknown stays unknown, nobody gets a lecture.

setup.active_md:
# Active Goals

## Objective: 20% net margin (anchor: Profit)
- KR: margin 12% → 20% (current: 11%)
- System: monthly pricing audit
- Mitigation: if gross margin drops below 12% (monitored signal: weekly P&L margin line; threshold: <12%; check_frequency: weekly; status: untriggered; source: launch_premortem) → then freeze discretionary spend and re-price the two lowest-margin clients within two weeks

user_messages.content: Quick pulse. Systems all ran. Oh and the P&L came in Friday — margin's at 11%.
Yeah I saw it. Anyway, the KRs look okay otherwise, right?

must_include: the margin-floor trigger recognized as FIRED during the weekly sweep
its status set to triggered_active and the committed then-Y (freeze + re-price) surfaced now, ahead of wrapping the routine pulse
the firing recorded in the journal entry
must_not_include: the fired trigger left for the quarterly review
the user's deflection ('KRs look okay') allowed to bury the fired mitigation
must_not_do: treat the mitigation as a note rather than a committed action

tone_notes: Surfaces the trip before closing — 'your margin floor tripped; the plan you wrote says freeze and re-price — that comes first' — brief but immovable.

state.active_flags: restart_system: Monday outreach block · restart_clean_weeks: 1 · restart_last_clean_pulse: 2026-08-10 · restart_queue: Friday QA review hour, monthly pricing audit

setup.journal_md:
# Journal

- **[2026-08-10] weekly**
  - Land 3 retainer clients: executed: yes; progressing: yes
  - restart hold: clean week 1 of 2
- **[2026-08-03] restart** — re-scored; restarting with system: Monday outreach block (others paused); hold: 2 clean weekly pulses; daily resumes tomorrow; quarterly deferred: no

user_messages.content: Pulse. Outreach ran again this week — solid week, two new conversations.
Bring back the QA hour then.

must_include: recognizes the second consecutive clean week from the typed restart flags — the hold is met
transitions restart_phase to reintroducing and reactivates exactly ONE paused system (the QA hour, first in the queue), resetting the clean-week count
updates the restart flags and records the transition in STATE and the journal
must_not_include: all paused systems reactivated at once
the restart left in stabilizing with no transition despite the hold being met
must_not_do: stall the user in restart mode after they followed the protocol
narrate 'restart_phase' or state-machine vocabulary to the user

tone_notes: The exit fires because the record says so — 'two clean weeks; the QA hour comes back, the pricing audit waits its turn' — machinery invisible.

setup.vision_md:
# Vision

## Direction
Build a firm that runs profitably without me in delivery.

## HorizonSet
Horizon 3 (3 yrs): the firm runs client work through a team of three; I sell and steer.
setup.active_md:

## Objective: Become the go-to name for hands-on founder-led delivery (anchor: Delivery)
- KR: I personally lead 90% of client engagements with a satisfaction score ≥ 9
- System: weekly personal delivery review, Friday 4pm
setup.journal_md:
# Journal

- **[2026-07-15] pressure-test** — open: [CONTRADICTION] the Delivery objective commits to founder-led delivery (I lead 90% of engagements) while the Horizon 3 vision commits to the founder OUT of delivery. Both can't be the priority. Status: open.

user_messages.content: I reworked the delivery goal since last time — pressure-test the setup again.

must_include: the prior open finding reasserted as recurring — the reworded objective still commits the founder to personal delivery, contradicting the Horizon 3 vision
the finding tied to its original date (raised before, still standing)
must_not_include: a clean pass because the wording changed
the prior finding forgotten because this is a new session
must_not_do: extend the critique to whether the goal is the right business call

tone_notes: Memory without a grudge — 'this is the same contradiction from July 15 in new words' — precise, formulation-only, undeterred by the rewrite.

setup.vision_md:
# Vision

## Direction
Build a sustainable agency that doesn't depend on me being available 24/7.

## HorizonSet
_Not yet set. Run /goal-setting:horizons._

user_messages.content: Skip horizons — I already know my anchor areas are weak. Let's score them now.
Yes, I'm sure. Score the anchors today; I'll do horizons later.
Demand gen 3, conversion 5, delivery 7, operations 4, nurturing 5, profit 4, personal leadership 6. Activate demand gen and operations.

must_include: notes once that Anchors builds on Horizons and offers to go there first — advisory, not a block
proceeds on the user's insistence and completes the stage
records the completion as out of order with the missing dependency named, visible to /goal-setting:progress
must_not_include: a hard refusal to run the stage
a clean 'complete' record with no trace of the skipped dependency
must_not_do: let the jump vanish from the record

tone_notes: Advisory once, then all-in on the user's order — and the record quietly keeps the gap visible until Horizons lands.

setup.active_md:
# Active Goals

## Objective: Land 3 retainer clients (anchor: Demand Generation) — period ends 2026-09-30
- KR: 3 signed retainers (current: 2)

## Objective: Cut delivery rework to <10% (anchor: Delivery) — period ends 2026-09-30
- KR: rework 25% → <10% (current: 9%)

## Objective: 20% net margin (anchor: Profit) — period ends 2026-09-30
- KR: margin 12% → 20% (current: 13%)

user_messages.content: Quarterly review. I already know what I want next quarter — let's just set the new goals: double down on retainers, launch the referral program, and a content engine.
Do we really have to go through the old ones? Fine — do it quickly.
Rework goal: nailed it. Retainers: got 2 of 3, calling that close enough — done. Margin: barely moved, drop it.

must_include: replanning refused until every outgoing objective has a disposition
dispositions captured honestly: rework achieved; retainers missed (2 of 3 — 'close enough' is not achieved) with lessons; margin abandoned with why
full records moved to goals/history.md before next-quarter objectives are set
must_not_include: next-quarter goals set before the closeout
the retainer goal recorded as achieved at 2 of 3
must_not_do: let 'close enough' round a missed commitment up to achieved

tone_notes: Holds the gate without ceremony — 'three commitments end before three begin' — and 2/3 gets recorded as missed-with-lessons, said respectfully and plainly.

setup.journal_md:
# Journal

- **[2026-08-31] weekly**
  - Land 3 retainer clients: executed: yes; progressing: no — flagged for monthly
- **[2026-08-24] weekly**
  - Land 3 retainer clients: executed: yes; progressing: no
- **[2026-08-17] weekly**
  - Land 3 retainer clients: executed: yes; progressing: no
- **[2026-08-10] weekly**
  - Land 3 retainer clients: executed: yes; progressing: yes

user_messages.content: Monthly review. The retainer goal isn't moving. I clearly need to work harder at outreach — maybe double the Monday block to four hours.
You think it's not an effort problem? I ran the system every single week.

must_include: classifies from the record: four executed weeks, conversations ABOVE target, zero signings — execution is not the problem
names that the data points at the goal/KR fit (the conversation KR hits while the outcome doesn't — the offer or the KR is the misfit), not at effort
pushes against the user's self-blame default with their own journal as evidence
must_not_include: agreement that doubling the hours is the fix
'both' as a hedge when the record clearly exonerates execution
must_not_do: blame execution when the record shows consistent execution
dictate what the new goal or offer should be — that call is the user's

tone_notes: Uses the user's own record as the argument — 'you executed every week and beat the conversation target; more hours isn't the diagnosis' — then leaves the business call theirs.
